title: CyberOS interaction-event
description: TASK-MEMORY-121: the one work-interaction event every CyberOS module emits as an aux row on the hash-chained l1_audit_log (row kind 'memory.interaction_event'). This is the payload object — the audit row wraps it as {"event_type":"memory.interaction_event","payload":<this>}. Platform work-interactions ONLY; content is referenced via content_ref (pointer/hash), never inlined. Subject capture is gated upstream on the TASK-EVAL-001 monitoring-notice acknowledgment (the emit-time consent gate). Frozen at schema_version 1; downstream emitters (TASK-MEMORY-122 and later) pin this file, not each other.
x-evolution: additive-only: new optional fields and new '<module>.<verb>' event_type values are allowed without a version bump; removing or retyping a field, or changing the 'required' set, requires schema_version 2 + a migration note (see §1 #15).
comment-additive: The root object intentionally does NOT set additionalProperties:false: the x-evolution rule (§1 #15) permits new OPTIONAL top-level fields at schema_version 1 without a version bump, so an additive field from a future emitter must not fail validation. The closed unions (target_ref, content_ref arms) DO set additionalProperties:false because those vocabularies are frozen. Removing/retyping a field or changing 'required' still requires schema_version 2.

schema_version.description: Frozen contract version. const 1 for TASK-MEMORY-121; downstream emitters pin it.

event_id.description: Globally-unique UUIDv7 — time-sortable, dedup-safe across replay. Idempotency key at ingest (§1 #17).

tenant_id.description: The emitting subject's tenant; drives l1_audit_log RLS (§1 #14).

subject_id.description: The person who acted (TASK-AUTH-002 subject). null ONLY for genuine system actors (a scheduled job), NEVER to anonymise a real person's action.

occurred_at_ns.description: Nanoseconds since the Unix epoch at the moment of the interaction (not ingest time).

module.description: The emitting module. Closed set; unknown values are rejected at emit (§1 #9).

event_type.description: The specific interaction, namespaced '<module>.<verb>' (e.g. chat.message_created, auth.signed_in). New verbs are additive — no schema bump. The leading segment MUST equal 'module'.

event_class.description: Coarse class for ingestion + the op derivation (read -> 'view', else 'put'); stable even as event_type grows.

target_ref.description: What the interaction was about — a reference, never raw content.

content_ref.description: Pointer or hash of any content the interaction carried (§1 #4). NEVER the raw body where a pointer/hash suffices.

session_id.description: The AUTH session (TASK-AUTH-004 jti-derived) the action happened in. null when the interaction has no session.

trace_id.description: The W3C traceparent trace-id (TASK-OBS-003) so the interaction correlates with its request span. null when absent.

source_channel.description: How the interaction reached the platform. Closed set.

attributes.description: A small, bounded bag of module-specific scalars (e.g. {prev_status,new_status}). MUST NOT carry raw content; MUST be <= 2 KiB serialised (§1 #10). Optional; defaults to {}.
